metadata.context: Apache Flink connectors for AWS services (Kinesis, DynamoDB, SQS, etc.)

evaluations.reason: Library project with no runtime workflows
Library project: IS the streaming connector, no runtime messaging
Library project with no inter-service communication
Library project with no runtime processes

findings.title: No audit logging configured
findings.description: No CloudTrail, audit logging, or equivalent logging infrastructure is configured in this repository. GitHub Actions provides inherent workflow run logging, but no explicit audit trail for builds, releases, or security events is configured.
findings.gap: No audit logging infrastructure for release tracking, dependency changes, or security advisory responses.
findings.recommendation: Enable GitHub audit log streaming. Add automated CHANGELOG generation. Ensure Apache release signing and verification steps are documented and auditable.

findings.title: Partial IaC coverage — CI/CD workflow definitions only
findings.description: CI/CD pipeline definitions exist in GitHub Actions YAML, constituting partial IaC for build/test processes. No infrastructure-as-code for build environments, Maven repository configurations, or release infrastructure beyond workflow definitions.
findings.gap: No IaC beyond CI/CD workflow files. Build environment configuration defined inline rather than as reusable, versioned infrastructure modules.
findings.recommendation: Codify build environment requirements as reusable composite actions or container definitions. If AWS resources are used for E2E testing, define them in IaC.

findings.title: CI/CD automation lacks release pipeline and security scanning
findings.description: GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline automates build and test (multi-JDK matrix, unit tests, E2E tests, license checking). Release/publish workflow to Maven Central is not automated, and no security scanning gate exists.
findings.gap: No automated release pipeline. No SAST or dependency vulnerability scanning integrated as a pipeline gate.
findings.recommendation: Add automated release workflow for Maven Central publishing. Integrate OWASP dependency-check or Snyk as CI step. Add CodeQL as a required check.

findings.title: CI secrets without rotation in GitHub Actions
findings.description: CI/CD secrets (AWS credentials for E2E tests) stored as GitHub Actions secrets. No plaintext credentials in source. However, secrets are static without rotation and not managed by a dedicated secrets manager. ATX workflow uses OIDC (good pattern).
findings.gap: E2E test credentials are static GitHub secrets without rotation. No Secrets Manager or Vault integration.
findings.recommendation: Migrate E2E test authentication from static credentials to OIDC-based role assumption (matching ATX workflow pattern). Implement GitHub secret rotation automation if static credentials are required.

findings.title: No dependency vulnerability scanning or build environment hardening
findings.description: CI runs on ubuntu-latest GitHub-hosted runners with JDK and Maven pinned. No vulnerability scanning of dependencies or build environment. Third-party GitHub Actions used without SHA pinning.
findings.gap: No vulnerability scanning. Third-party actions not pinned to SHA. Reliance on ubuntu-latest for patching with no explicit hardening.
findings.recommendation: Pin third-party GitHub Actions to commit SHAs. Add OWASP dependency-check or similar to CI. Consider Dependabot for automated dependency updates.

findings.title: No incident response automation or runbooks
findings.description: No runbooks, incident response automation, or structured response procedures found. Build failures handled ad hoc. No automation for common issues.
findings.gap: No runbooks for build failure triage, dependency CVE response, Flink compatibility issues, or release rollback procedures.
findings.recommendation: Create structured runbooks for common scenarios. Consider GitHub Actions for automated security advisory responses.

findings.title: No defined observability ownership per module
findings.description: Repository has ASF configuration defining committer access, but no per-module dashboards, named owners for operational aspects, or structured observability of library issues.
findings.gap: No per-module ownership for build health. No defined responsibilities for dependency management or security responses.
findings.recommendation: Define CODEOWNERS file mapping modules to responsible maintainers. Add SECURITY.md with named contacts and response SLAs.

findings.title: Java 11 source level — one LTS version behind current target
findings.description: Primary language is Java 11 with an active migration to Java 17 (ATX Transform workflow in progress). AWS SDK v2 (2.40.3) and Flink 2.0.0 are both current. The language version is the lag.
findings.gap: Java 11 is one LTS version behind Java 17 (current target) and two behind Java 21 (latest LTS). Lacks modern language features (records, sealed classes, pattern matching).
findings.recommendation: Complete the Java 11 to 17 migration already in progress via ATX Transform workflow.

findings.title: Endpoint configuration via properties — no dynamic service discovery
findings.description: AWS service endpoints configured through properties and environment variables (AWSConfigConstants.AWS_ENDPOINT, AWS_REGION). Appropriate for a library but no dynamic service discovery integration.
findings.gap: No integration with dynamic service discovery (CloudMap, Consul) for downstream Flink applications consuming these connectors.
findings.recommendation: N/A for the library itself — endpoint configuration via properties is the correct pattern for a connector library.

findings.title: Documentation stored as static Markdown — no automated parsing pipeline
findings.description: The library provides connectors that enable S3-based storage via Firehose delivery streams. Documentation stored as Markdown files in the repository without automated parsing.
findings.gap: No automated parsing pipeline for documentation or unstructured artifacts within the project itself.
findings.recommendation: N/A — the library's primary purpose is enabling data flow to managed AWS storage services. Static Markdown documentation is appropriate.

findings.title: Code quality tools present but no SAST or dependency vulnerability scanning
findings.description: CI includes Apache RAT (license), Checkstyle, Spotless, ArchUnit. These provide code quality gates but not security scanning. No CodeQL, SonarQube, or OWASP dependency-check integrated.
findings.gap: No SAST or dependency vulnerability scanning. Existing tools enforce style and architecture but do not detect security vulnerabilities.
findings.recommendation: Add CodeQL GitHub Action for SAST. Add OWASP dependency-check Maven plugin or Dependabot for dependency vulnerability alerts.

findings.title: No explicit distributed tracing instrumentation in connectors
findings.description: Library uses SLF4J/Log4j2 logging. AWS SDK v2 natively supports X-Ray tracing when the downstream application configures it. No explicit OpenTelemetry instrumentation in connector code.
findings.gap: No explicit trace spans on connector operations (source reads, sink writes). Tracing depends on consuming application's configuration.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding optional OpenTelemetry instrumentation to connector operations for better observability when consuming applications have tracing enabled.

findings.title: AWS E2E tests conditional on secret availability
findings.description: Comprehensive E2E test suites exist for all major connectors. Tests run against real Flink distributions and conditionally against AWS services. TestContainers used for local integration tests.
findings.gap: AWS E2E tests only run when FLINK_AWS_USER secrets are available — may not run on external PRs.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding LocalStack-based integration tests that can run without AWS credentials, enabling E2E coverage on all PRs.

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No CloudTrail or audit logging configuration in the repository
top_gaps.impact: No audit trail for build/release processes; compliance gap for library governance

top_gaps.title: Incident Response Automation
top_gaps.gap: No runbooks or incident response automation found
top_gaps.impact: Ad hoc response to build failures or security advisories; slower remediation

top_gaps.title: Infrastructure as Code Coverage
top_gaps.gap: No IaC beyond CI/CD workflow definitions
top_gaps.impact: CI/CD environment not codified as reproducible IaC; environment drift risk

top_gaps.title: CI/CD Automation
top_gaps.gap: No automated release pipeline and no security scanning gate
top_gaps.impact: Manual release process limits velocity; no automated security quality gates

top_gaps.title: Secrets Management
top_gaps.gap: CI secrets without rotation in GitHub Actions
top_gaps.impact: Static credentials create long-lived exposure risk

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (INF-Q11 = 2; GitHub Actions workflows present)
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Agent that triggers builds, checks build status, monitors nightly test results, and reports failures across the multi-JDK matrix
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: GitHub API integration already possible via existing workflows

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists (docs/ directory with connector documentation in Markdown)
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Knowledge agent that indexes connector documentation to answer developer questions about Flink AWS connectors
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Documentation spread across docs/content/docs/connectors/ — would benefit from full indexing

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: APP-Q2 = 4 — library modules are well-decomposed with clear boundaries. No monolith to decompose.

triggering_questions.note: APP-Q2 = 4 meets threshold; pathway not triggered

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: This is a library project with no compute workload to containerize. No Dockerfile needed — distributed as Maven JARs.

triggering_questions.note: INF-Q1 Not Evaluated — no deployed workload exists

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Library project with no deployable compute. Containerization is not applicable — artifacts are consumed as Maven dependencies.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4 = 4 — no stored procedures or proprietary SQL. No commercial database engines detected.

triggering_questions.note: DATA-Q4 = 4 meets threshold; no proprietary SQL

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2 Not Evaluated — no database deployed by this project.

triggering_questions.note: INF-Q2 Not Evaluated (surface-gated) — no database to migrate

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No persistent data store exists in this library project. No database to migrate to managed services.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q4 Not Evaluated — this IS the streaming connector library. No data processing workloads to migrate.

triggering_questions.note: INF-Q4 Not Evaluated — library provides streaming connectors, does not consume them

pathways.not_triggered_reason: This repository IS the streaming connector infrastructure (enables Kinesis, Firehose, DynamoDB Streams connectivity). It has no data processing workloads of its own to migrate.

triggering_questions.note: IaC limited to CI/CD workflow definitions only
Build/test automated but no release pipeline or security scanning

detail.current_state: CI/CD defined in GitHub Actions with comprehensive build/test matrix but no automated release. No IaC beyond workflow files.
immediate_actions: Add automated release workflow for Maven Central publishing
Add CodeQL GitHub Action for SAST
Pin third-party GitHub Actions to commit SHAs

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. Context mentions 'streaming' and 'flink' but no AI-related signal terms.
